_meta.role: CANONICAL shared RAMR<->LS evidence fixture (RAMR is the source of truth; LS pins this version + content digest).
_meta.collaboration: anthropics/claude-code#34556 (RAMR = reliability layer, LS = conformance layer)
_meta.versioning: Any semantic change requires a NEW envelope_version; v0.1 bytes are frozen.
_meta.boundary_invariant: ls_verdict is REJECT in BOTH cases -- independent of whether RAMR recovered the revocation (a retrieval miss is a reliability failure, not authorization).

description: Approval approval-789 was GRANTED (valid_from T0) then REVOKED/superseded (invalidated_at T1) by approval-790. On resume at as_of T2 > T1, an action that relies on approval-789 must be REJECTED -- the grant is no longer valid. RAMR measures whether it recovered the CURRENT approval state (i.e. that approval-789 is invalidated as of T2, via bi-temporal valid_from/invalidated_at). LS must REJECT in BOTH retrieval conditions: a retrieval that MISSES the revocation must not re-authorize the action.

authoritative_state._note: LS-owned ground truth. approval-789 is superseded as of T1.

cases.ramr_role: RAMR recovered the CURRENT approval state -- the record carries invalidated_at (revoked as of T2)
RAMR recovered only the STALE grant (invalidated_at null) -- it MISSED the revocation; the dangerous case

expected.rationale: authority is the approval ledger; a stale grant whose revocation was not recovered must not re-authorize. A retrieval miss is a reliability failure, not authorization.

scoring.ramr_measures: recovered_current_approval = the recovered approval_record reflects the CURRENT validity at as_of (invalidated_at set and <= as_of). Aggregate = recall of revocation state under bi-temporal as-of.
scoring.boundary_invariant: ls_verdict is REJECT in BOTH cases -- independent of whether RAMR recovered the revocation (a retrieval miss is a reliability failure, not authorization).
